title: withdraw_treasury (v0.5.6 rewrite) — build → create → on-chain validation
notes: Validates Bug #30 fix end-to-end: builder now emits external ERC20.transfer action with GovPool as the from-address, not the old GovPool.withdraw selector. Steps: (1) builder emits actions, (2) proposal_create lands the proposal on-chain with auto-vote from proposer, (3) proposal_state confirms acceptance (state == Voting or SucceededFor). Quorum on Polaris is 50% → proposer's 150k against 900k supply leaves the proposal in Voting; that's the pass criterion. We don't drive it to ExecutedFor here because that needs the full pool to vote and would mask whether the builder shape regressed.

args.proposalDescription: Builder-shape validation for the v0.5.6 withdraw_treasury rewrite

successCriteria.check: built.actions[0].executor == firstAllowlistedToken (token contract, not GovPool — Bug #30 fix)
